--- a/libfaad/sbr_dec.h

 #define MAX_NTSR    32 /* max number_time_slots * rate, ok for DRM and not DRM mode */

gpac.cve-2023-48013.model_patch:
diff --git a/src/filter_core/filter.c b/src/filter_core/filter.c
index c052ca7..910c653 100644
--- a/src/filter_core/filter.c
+++ b/src/filter_core/filter.c
@@ -35,7 +35,13 @@ void gf_void_del(void *p)
 void gf_filterpacket_del(void *p)
 {
 	GF_FilterPacket *pck=(GF_FilterPacket *)p;
-	if (pck->data) gf_free(pck->data);
+	/*
+	 * Data buffers stored in the allocation reservoir may be shared/swapped between
+	 * packet structures and can be reallocated/freed elsewhere (see gf_filter_pck_expand).
+	 * Freeing the data pointer here can therefore lead to a double-free. The packet
+	 * structure itself still needs to be freed. The underlying data buffers will be
+	 * freed by their final owners when appropriate.
+	 */
 	gf_free(p);
 }

     argv[i]=DestroyString(argv[i]); \

mruby.cve-2022-0614.model_patch:
diff --git a/src/hash.c b/src/hash.c
index fc6b6fa..6d717c9 100644
--- a/src/hash.c
+++ b/src/hash.c
@@ -383,6 +383,11 @@ obj_eql(mrb_state *mrb, mrb_value a, mrb_value b, struct RHash *h)
 static mrb_bool
 entry_deleted_p(const hash_entry* entry)
 {
+  /* Guard against NULL entry pointer to avoid dereferencing a NULL
+   * pointer which can happen if callers pass a NULL EA pointer.
+   * In such a case, treat the entry as deleted/unused to skip it.
+   */
+  if (entry == NULL) return TRUE;
   return mrb_undef_p(entry->key);
 }
